pairs.bug_description: The buggy solution incorrectly computes pairs (p, q) by manipulating bits instead of ensuring that p & q = 0. This results in invalid pairs that fail to minimize the distance as required, leading to a wrong answer.
pairs.problem_statement:
# B. Shortest Statement Ever

**Time limit:** 2 seconds
**Memory limit:** 256 megabytes

## Problem Statement

You are given two non-negative integers $$$x$$$, $$$y$$$. Find two non-negative integers $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$ such that $$$p\\;\\&\\;q=0$$$, and $$$|x-p|+|y-q|$$$ is minimized. Here, $$$\\&$$$ denotes the [bitwise AND operation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bitwise_operation#AND).

## Input

Each test contains multiple test cases. The first line contains the number of test cases $$$t$$$ ($$$1 \\le t \\le 10^4$$$). The description of the test cases follows.

The only line of each test case contains two non-negative integers $$$x$$$ and $$$y$$$ ($$$0 \\le x,y \\lt 2^{30}$$$).

## Output

For each test case, output two non-negative integers $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$ you found. If there are multiple pairs of $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$ satisfying the conditions, you may output any of them.

It can be proven that under the constraints of the problem, any valid solution satisfies $$$\\max(p,q) \\lt 2^{31}$$$.

## Sample Tests

### Input
```
7
0 0
1 1
3 6
7 11
4 4
123 321
1073741823 1073741822
```

### Output
```
0 0
2 1
3 8
6 9
4 3
128 321
1073741824 1073741822
```

## Note

In the first test case, one valid pair would be $$$p=0$$$ and $$$q=0$$$, as $$$0\\,\\&\\,0=0$$$ and $$$|x-p|+|y-q|=|0-0|+|0-0|=0$$$ is the minimum over all pairs of $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$.

In the third test case, one valid pair would be $$$p=3$$$ and $$$q=8$$$, as $$$3\\,\\&\\,8=0$$$ and $$$|x-p|+|y-q|=|3-3|+|8-6|=2$$$ is the minimum over all pairs of $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$. Note that $$$(p,q)=(3,4)$$$ is also a valid pair.

pairs.bug_description: The buggy solution has logical errors in the calculations of `new_p` and `new_q`, particularly with how it handles the bitwise operations, which leads to incorrect results for minimizing the distance while satisfying the AND condition.
pairs.problem_statement:

pairs.bug_description: The buggy solution incorrectly determines the pairs (p, q) based on wrong conditional logic when checking for the minimized absolute differences, leading to incorrect outputs for certain test cases.

# B. Shortest Statement Ever

**Time limit:** 2 seconds
**Memory limit:** 256 megabytes

## Problem Statement

You are given two non-negative integers $$$x$$$, $$$y$$$. Find two non-negative integers $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$ such that $$$p\\;\\&\\;q=0$$$, and $$$|x-p|+|y-q|$$$ is minimized. Here, $$$\\&$$$ denotes the [bitwise AND operation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bitwise_operation#AND).

## Input

Each test contains multiple test cases. The first line contains the number of test cases $$$t$$$ ($$$1 \\le t \\le 10^4$$$). The description of the test cases follows.

The only line of each test case contains two non-negative integers $$$x$$$ and $$$y$$$ ($$$0 \\le x,y \\lt 2^{30}$$$).

## Output

For each test case, output two non-negative integers $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$ you found. If there are multiple pairs of $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$ satisfying the conditions, you may output any of them.

It can be proven that under the constraints of the problem, any valid solution satisfies $$$\\max(p,q) \\lt 2^{31}$$$.

## Sample Tests

### Input
```

0 0
1 1
3 6
7 11
4 4
123 321
1073741823 1073741822
```

### Output
```
0 0
2 1
3 8
6 9
4 3
128 321
1073741824 1073741822
```

## Note

In the first test case, one valid pair would be $$$p=0$$$ and $$$q=0$$$, as $$$0\\,\\&\\,0=0$$$ and $$$|x-p|+|y-q|=|0-0|+|0-0|=0$$$ is the minimum over all pairs of $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$.

In the third test case, one valid pair would be $$$p=3$$$ and $$$q=8$$$, as $$$3\\,\\&\\,8=0$$$ and $$$|x-p|+|y-q|=|3-3|+|8-6|=2$$$ is the minimum over all pairs of $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$. Note that $$$(p,q)=(3,4)$$$ is also a valid pair.

pairs.bug_description: The buggy solution incorrectly modifies the logic in the 'tryi' function by trying all combinations of 'a' and 'b' without specifying the correct minimization strategy, leading to output pairs that do not yield the minimal distance as intended.

Specifically, it uses curp and curq when accessing pres without correctly updating them for each iteration, which can cause wrong conditional logic.


## Note

In the first test case, one valid pair would be $$$p=0$$$ and $$$q=0$$$, as $$$0\\,\\&\\,0=0$$$ and $$$|x-p|+|y-q|=|0-0|+|0-0|=0$$$ is the minimum over all pairs of $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$.

In the third test case, one valid pair would be $$$p=3$$$ and $$$q=8$$$, as $$$3\\,\\&\\,8=0$$$ and $$$|x-p|+|y-q|=|3-3|+|8-6|=2$$$ is the minimum over all pairs of $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$. Note that $$$(p,q)=(3,4)$$$ is also a valid pair.

pairs.bug_description: The buggy solution incorrectly calculates the optimal values of p and q by not fully considering the implications of the bitwise AND operation and how it affects the minimization of the distance, leading to a greedy approach that does not always yield the correct minimum.
pairs.problem_statement:
# B. Shortest Statement Ever

**Time limit:** 2 seconds
**Memory limit:** 256 megabytes

## Problem Statement

You are given two non-negative integers $$$x$$$, $$$y$$$. Find two non-negative integers $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$ such that $$$p\\;\\&\\;q=0$$$, and $$$|x-p|+|y-q|$$$ is minimized. Here, $$$\\&$$$ denotes the [bitwise AND operation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bitwise_operation#AND).

## Input

Each test contains multiple test cases. The first line contains the number of test cases $$$t$$$ ($$$1 \\le t \\le 10^4$$$). The description of the test cases follows.

The only line of each test case contains two non-negative integers $$$x$$$ and $$$y$$$ ($$$0 \\le x,y \\lt 2^{30}$$$).

## Output

For each test case, output two non-negative integers $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$ you found. If there are multiple pairs of $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$ satisfying the conditions, you may output any of them.

It can be proven that under the constraints of the problem, any valid solution satisfies $$$\\max(p,q) \\lt 2^{31}$$$.

## Sample Tests

### Input
```
7
0 0
1 1
3 6
7 11
4 4
123 321
1073741823 1073741822
```

### Output
```
0 0
2 1
3 8
6 9
4 3
128 321
1073741824 1073741822
```

## Note

In the first test case, one valid pair would be $$$p=0$$$ and $$$q=0$$$, as $$$0\\,\\&\\,0=0$$$ and $$$|x-p|+|y-q|=|0-0|+|0-0|=0$$$ is the minimum over all pairs of $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$.

In the third test case, one valid pair would be $$$p=3$$$ and $$$q=8$$$, as $$$3\\,\\&\\,8=0$$$ and $$$|x-p|+|y-q|=|3-3|+|8-6|=2$$$ is the minimum over all pairs of $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$. Note that $$$(p,q)=(3,4)$$$ is also a valid pair.
